Review_Text: EA Sports FC 25 is a fantastic evolution of the football simulation series, offering a more realistic and rewarding experience than ever before. The slower, tactical gameplay makes matches feel authentic, giving players the chance to build plays with precision rather than relying on arcade-style speed. Career mode has been expanded with smarter AI, deeper team management, and more meaningful rivalries, making progression feel immersive and personal. The addition of Hypermotion V and FC IQ technology enhances animations and player behavior, creating lifelike movement and smarter decision-making on the pitch. Palm City may not be here, but the stadiums and atmosphere are vibrant, capturing the thrill of real football. While minor bugs and clunky menus remain, the overall package is polished, strategic, and endlessly fun. FC 25 is a must-play for fans who want football that feels closer to the real thing.

The only noticeable change is the price, which feels ridiculously high for what the game actually offers. A few minor tweaks were made here and there, but everything else remains exactly the same.

What deserves special mention are the unbearable lags in the main menu and the incredibly long loading times. Older entries never had such issues, yet here it feels like the developers intentionally locked the game to 10 FPS, forcing you to wait several seconds after almost every action.

Overall, the game simply isn't worth its price - unless this is your very first time wanting to play a football game on PC or console.

Review_Text:
I am heavily disappointed with FC 25 and I do NOT recommend anyone to buy this game or any future EA FC product unless the developers come forward and properly optimize it.
One match runs smooth, the next feels choppy, laggy, and completely unresponsive. Defending becomes impossible, passing is delayed, and even scoring feels like a struggle. It doesn’t feel like skill vs skill anymore — it feels like the game engine is fighting against the player.
On top of that, there are no proper dedicated servers in many regions, leading to inconsistent gameplay, input delay, and unfair matches.
This is unacceptable for a so-called “next-gen” football game from a billion-dollar company.
Until this game is rebuilt and optimized properly, this product deserves to be boycotted.
Do not buy. Do not support. Fix the game first.

Review_Text:
I've played FIFA and subsequent FC titles since the N64. I've grit my teeth as their emulation of the actual sport has steadily declined over the last decade. This one represents the threshold at which I can no longer force myself to play. 

It's hollow, cumbersome and now an utter chore to put up with. All the usual bugs are still there, while achieving a further regression in the playing experience overall. The menu interface is utter garbage. The gameplay design and AI is genuinely beyond the pale. I fear FC26 will likely be the same story.

I'm easily impressed mind you. I'd be more than happy with player models made with just a dozen polygons, some Fatboy Slim playing in crisp, straight-forward menus, and a design team with at least some understanding of the sport. These days, though, the franchise is simply geared towards kids from a specific region of the world, as this represents the biggest micro-transaction market. 

We've had some great times over the decades old mate.
Sad to see you go.

Review_Text:
C 25 isn’t that bad either, but at the same time, it’s definitely not the huge EA “we reinvented football” whisper everyone has been hearing about all along. The gameplay has been made a bit more realistic – now your players won’t spin like Beyblades – but still, EA Sports doesn’t deprive you of those legendary moments when your defender politely lets your opponent pass through, saying “after you, pal.”

Passing, you know, is a good thing, but then again, good passing, but not good enough, it’s just disastrous, you know? One minute you’re playing tik-tak, and the next, your midfielder is kicking a ball like it’s headed for a NASA rocket through the centre-back of the other team.

Career Mode? Minor tweakage, but hardly anything to scream “wow.” Ultimate Team remains Ultimate Team—that is, it’s a blast, highly addictive, although somehow draining your wallet and soul simultaneously. Pro Clubs? Absolutely one of the better aspects, primarily if done with friends, in which case laughing at glitches is literally half the game.

Is there fulfillment of the expectations? Honestly — yes, and just. It's like it's upgraded from last year's model and not new and improved. If you are a fan of football games, then of course you'll play it. If you've been AWOL during the last launch of the game and missed it, then no worries. If you've been hoping for a change. then no way.

Review_Text:
This review is only towards the career mode in FC25!

I won't waste your time by not telling you straight forward if the game is worth ( if you're buying it only for the career mode ). It is NOT!

CONS:
- After a couple of games you'd get the chance of giving an interview. You have to choose between 4 different options as answers on 3 questions that repeat themselfs and all of this only to boost a little bit of the morale on your players.

- If you play manager career and you're staying at the same team each season, your salary stays the same and it doesn't matter if you've claimed from League 2 to Premier League while other clubs that offer you a job also offer you x5 as much money as you're currently earning. ( Keep in mind that earning money as a manager does absolutely nothing but it would've been a nice and more realistic detail ).

- While negotiating on loaning one of your players you have the chance to debate on what % of that player salary you're willing to pay and 95% of the time it's 60/40 and the other 5% is 50/50. The game is coded in a way that allows you to lower it to 60/40 in your favour but not less than that.

- If you're negotiating on loaning one of your players and you pay attention on your budget you'll see that the less you pay the lower the budget becomes even though there's 0 logic behind it and math does not work that way.

- Players complain often and you have only 3 choices to answer with. 1 boosts their morale, 1 boosts it a little or it stays the same as it was and the last one lowers their morale. Nothing else changes.

- Youth academy overall potential is only visible while you're participating in tournaments. They should've made it visible in the youth squad as well to make it twice as easy and less time consuming to check it mostly because youth tournaments are useless as they do not boost the player developement.

- Player photos on EA generated players should've been updated by now. They're the same as in FC22/23/24 etc. We're at the point where AI can generate random profile images making it much easier to recognize players by their look not by checking OV, age or the position. Currently the random generated players have very similar look and are harder to tell apart when you open your whole squad.

- The comentators have 5 lines that are being repeated over and over and over again and it makes you turn them off. They should've added AI to the game with dynamic comentating. It is not unthinkible as we've seen similar stuff in other video games.

- There's nothing else to do apart from playing the matches, developing your youth squad and buying/selling players. As a manager they should've added something that makes the game a bit more fun and realistic. Stuff like market to boost your manager appearence with the money you've earned, clotches etc.

WHAT THEY COULD'VE ADDED TO MAKE THE GAME DIFFERENT FROM PREVIOUS FIFA GAMES AND MORE FUN:

- As mentioned above they could've added market, clotches or anything related to you as a manager to change your appearance with the money you've earned.

 - More options to answer the players that are complaining or just messaging you.

- Voice over when you're giving an interview before or after a game. Right now there's no voice over and you just see how your manager is moving his lips while giving an answer but no voice.

- More and different images on the EA generated players.

- Youth academy max potential inside the youth squad instead of having to participate in a tournament just to check it.

- More lines for the comentators or just use dynamic AI.

- When you're getting the manager of the year award or if your players are getting player of the season for example it's just a cinematic with no audio at all. They should've added some voice over when the award and winner are announced and at least a single line in response. Or anything different than what is now because it makes you skip it every single time. They could've also make it more interesting if you're just invited to that event with 2-3 more managers from different teams and all of you are nominated for manager of the year and you get to hear which one of you got the award.

I'll updated the review in the near future as I'm bored and too lazy to continue writing a review that will not change the game in any way as EA does not care about it. All they care about is the Ultimate team and how to get your money on the packs they're selling. #DontBuyIt

Review_Text: EA Sports FC 25 is a fantastic evolution of the football simulation series, offering a more realistic and rewarding experience than ever before. The slower, tactical gameplay makes matches feel authentic, giving players the chance to build plays with precision rather than relying on arcade-style speed. Career mode has been expanded with smarter AI, deeper team management, and more meaningful rivalries, making progression feel immersive and personal. The addition of Hypermotion V and FC IQ technology enhances animations and player behavior, creating lifelike movement and smarter decision-making on the pitch. Palm City may not be here, but the stadiums and atmosphere are vibrant, capturing the thrill of real football. While minor bugs and clunky menus remain, the overall package is polished, strategic, and endlessly fun. FC 25 is a must-play for fans who want football that feels closer to the real thing.
